en: Challenge! How many phrasal verbs with call do you know?
ko: 도전! call이 들어간 구동사를 몇 개나 알아?
지식을 테스트해보자. 1번. 전화로 얘기했던 사람에게 다시 전화해야 하면

en: Number 1. If you need to phone someone who you spoke to on the phone earlier, you call them back.
Hi, sorry, can I call you back? I'm just about to have my lunch.
ko: 안녕, 미안한데 나중에 다시 전화해도 될까? 지금 점심 먹으려던 참이야.

en: Number 2. When we need to cancel an event or activity, we call it off.
I'm afraid we'll have to call off the team meeting. Yeah, I'm stuck in traffic.
ko: call it off야. 팀 회의를 취소해야 할 것 같아. 교통 체증에 갇혀 있어.

en: Number 3. If someone criticises someone's behaviour publicly, we can say they call them out.
I don't think Andrew was stuck in traffic earlier. I think he was lying. I'm going to call him out about it at the next meeting.
ko: call them out이야. Andrew가 아까 교통 체증에 갇혀 있었던 게 아닌 것 같아.

en: If you want to learn more useful vocabulary, follow us at BBC Learning English.
Number 4. When a person or a group of people demand for something publicly, they call for it.
I don't think you were stuck in traffic earlier.
Okay, I wasn't, but I don't like coming into the office every day. I call for more relaxed working conditions.
ko: 아까 교통 체증에 갇혀 있었던 게 아니었잖아. 맞아, 아니었어. 근데 매일 출근하는 게 싫어.

en: Lastly, number 5. If you visit someone briefly, you call in on them.
I hope Andrew's okay. I should probably call in on him on my way home.
ko: call in on이야. Andrew 괜찮은지 모르겠어. 퇴근길에 들러봐야겠다.

en: So, did you know all of these phrasal verbs with call? If you did, well done!
ko: call이 들어간 구동사를 다 알았어? 맞혔으면 잘했어.

en: If not, don't worry. Save this video and make it part of your English revision.
ko: 아니어도 괜찮아. 이 영상을 저장해서 영어 복습에 활용해.
